Continuity revalidation waiver checklist summary

Summary card that should be dropped.

Continuity revalidation waiver checklist

Continuity revalidation waiver checklists matter when operators need a deterministic sequence for deciding whether the default revalidation flow can be skipped.

The checklist explains how review checkpoints, queue health evidence, and fallback queues should be checked before a waiver path is reused.

Teams are expected to preserve fairness controls, handoff records, and recovery evidence so waiver decisions remain traceable across shifts.

Longer incidents still require explicit rollback ownership and a fresh approval window before a waiver path is extended.
